REMOVED FROM ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR ON JUN-20-2013. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 503 ON MAR-24-2014 AT 11:15 AM FOR TRIAL. THE COURT AND COUNSELS CONFER REGARDING EXPERT WITNESS DEMANDS AND EXPERT WITNESS DEPOSITION NOTICES AS NOTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT. DEMAND FOR EXPERTS SHALL BE SIMULTANEOUS EXCHANGES 50 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL AND ALL THE REPORTS PURSUANT TO CODE. EACH SIDE INFORM THE OTHER 35 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL WHICH EXPERTS THEY ARE GOING TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF AND THEN SCHEDULE THE DEPOSITION. PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM BY BERRY & BERRY. LIV/ASB (BRAYTON). JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: KIM RICHARDSON, CSR#5915 FROM AIKEN WELCH (510)451-1580 FOR DESIGNATED DEFENSE COUNSEL BERRY & BERRY. (206)
ORDER TRIAL SETTING ORDER RE EXPERT WITNESS DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 53881291)

MASTER JURY ASBESTOS CALENDAR OF MAR-24-2014 ASSIGNED TO ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL CALENDAR ON APR-07-2014 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 611, VISITING JUDGE LYNN O'MALLEY-TAYLOR. PLAINTIFF EXERCISED A 170.6 AS TO JUDGE MARLA MILLER AND THE COURT ACCEPTED THE CHALLENGE AS TIMELY. DEFENDANT OWENS ILLINOIS EXERCISED A 170.6 CCP PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE NEWTON LAM. THE COURT ACCEPTED THE CHALLENGE AS TIMELY. THE COURT ORDERED PARTIES TO HAVE A LIMIT OF 5 MOTIONS IN LIMINE PER EACH SIDE THAT ARE NOT TO EXCEED 5 PAGES. THE PLAINTIFF IS ALSO REQUESTING DEFENDANTS TO HAVE A COMPREHENSIVE INDEX. IF A DEFENDANT NEEDS A CASE SPECIFIC OR DEFENDANT SPECIFIC MIL IN ADDITION TO THE 5 MIL'S THAT ARE BEING FILED JOINTLY, PARTIES NEED TO MEET AND CONFER WITH FELLOW CO-DEFENDANTS AND THEN MEET AND CONFER WITH PLAINTIFF, THEN SUBMIT A DECLARATION BY 3/28/14 AT 9:00AM IN DEPT. 503 WITH ALL OF THE MOTIONS IN LIMINE FILED AS WELL. PARTIES NEED TO WORK ON THE PAGE LINE DESIGNATIONS AND SUBMIT A JOINT LETTER TO THE COURT REGARDING THE STATUS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: RVELUZ; COURT REPORTER: JENNIFER SEELENBACHER, CSR#11878, (510)451-1580. (503)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Sonia Wong, CSR#11288.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, GUNTHER NEUMANN.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiff GUNTHER NEUMANN shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $50,000.00 in Economic Damages, $300,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $350,000.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
